steps.title: Identify the core claim about quantities
steps.content: The astronomers specifically state that planets contain “equal or smaller quantities” of the materials found in their host stars.

steps.title: Find evidence that directly contradicts this relationship
steps.content: Choice C directly contradicts the claim by showing a rocky planet contains considerably more iron than its host star. The others reinforce the shared-materials foundation (A, B) or question methodology for non-rocky planets (D), neither addressing the core quantity relationship. That matches choice C — C is correct.
